asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover for Home Assistant Matter and Thread in a Proxmox virtual machine
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape editorial photograph
Primary request: a clean, realistic smart-home infrastructure scene for an article about running Home Assistant Matter and Thread inside a Proxmox virtual machine
Scene/backdrop: a warm lived-in residential utility nook beside a quiet home office, with a compact fanless mini PC on a natural oak shelf, a small simple Ethernet switch or router, one dedicated unbranded Thread USB radio on a short USB extension cable placed clearly away from the computer, and one small unbranded smart plug and contact sensor nearby as context
Style/medium: photorealistic editorial interior photography, natural everyday detail, not a product render and not a diagram
Composition/framing: 3:2 landscape, medium-wide eye-level view; mini PC, Ethernet connection, and USB radio remain recognizable in the central card crop; uncluttered breathing room; no prominent computer display
Lighting/mood: soft morning window light, calm dependable practical mood
Color palette: warm cream and oak with restrained dark-green accents in a cable tie and small fabric detail
Materials/textures: believable brushed metal, matte plastic, woven cable, oak grain, slight everyday imperfections
Constraints: physically plausible cables and ports; unbranded generic hardware only; no people except possibly a natural hand adjusting one cable; no text, no logos, no watermark
Avoid: embedded headline or title poster, legible UI or fake software screen, fake chart or graph, decorative routing/network/path line overlays, glowing connection lines, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, diagrams, split-screen composition, sci-fi lighting, excessive beige device lineup, invented branded product hardware, glossy CGI look
